title: 10 best herbal capsules for neck pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease stiffness in the muscles and joints. The active compound glycyrrhizin in licorice root has a profound effect on relieving pain and discomfort associated with neck strain or tension headaches. Additionally, licorice capsules can help relax the muscles, promoting better posture and reducing muscle spasms that contribute to chronic neck pain. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal licorice capsules provide effective relief for those suffering from neck pain.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Reduces inflammation and relieves pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which can contribute to neck pain and stiffness.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate neck pain and discomfort.
Saponins: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the neck and surrounding muscles.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with neck pain.

Licopyrone A (also known as Glycyrrhetol): Licopyrone A has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and regulating the body's response to pain.
Isoliquiritigenin: Isoliquiritigenin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation that may contribute to neck pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in glycyrrhizic acid, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
Leaves: They contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that contribute to pain relief and muscle relaxation.
Barks: They contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a bitter sweet flavor and a rich history of use.
Grind 10 grams of the harvested roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 50 grams of gelatin and 20 grams of microcrystalline cellulose in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powder mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to maintain their potency and freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with neck pain because they provide a natural, targeted approach to relieving tension and inflammation. The herbs contained within the capsules, such as turmeric, ginger, and willow bark, work together to reduce stiffness and promote relaxation in the muscles of the neck and shoulders. By increasing blood flow and reducing swelling, these herbal remedies can help alleviate chronic pain and discomfort associated with conditions like arthritis, fibromyalgia, and strain injuries.
remedy_properties: Turmeric's Curcumin: Reduces inflammation and alleviates pain, as it has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that target the root causes of neck pain.
Ginger's Gingerols: Relaxes muscles and reduces spasms, as ginger's active compounds help to relax tight muscles and reduce muscle tension in the neck region.

Boswellia's Boswellic Acids: Inhibits pro-inflammatory enzymes and reduces joint pain, as boswellia's key components inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals that contribute to neck pain and stiffness.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), help with neck pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected area, promoting relaxation and pain relief.
Theaflavins: Theaflavins, a type of flavonoid, help with neck pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and pain.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties, helps with neck pain by blocking the activation of pain-sensing nerve cells and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, leading to pain relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high content of caffeine and theobromine, which provide a relaxing and soothing effect for neck pain.
Roots: Roots are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with neck pain.
Barks: Barks are utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation, promoting relaxation and soothing the neck area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest camellia sinensis leaves in the early morning when they are at their highest antioxidant levels.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered camellia sinensis with 250 milligrams of a natural binder in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the herbal properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo capsules helps with neck pain because they contain a unique blend of herbs that target the root causes of discomfort. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and ease muscle tension, allowing for greater range of motion and reduced stiffness in the neck. Additionally, Yanhusuo's ability to promote blood circulation and relax the muscles helps to alleviate strain on the cervical spine, providing long-lasting relief from chronic neck pain.
remedy_properties: Corydaline: Relaxes muscles and reduces spasms, which helps to alleviate neck pain caused by muscle tension or strain.
Berberine: Has anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain in the neck area, and can also help to improve blood circulation and reduce stiffness.
Cryptopleurine: Has analgesic properties, providing relief from acute neck pain by blocking pain signals to the brain and promoting a sense of relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (specifically, dehydrocorybulbine and tetrahydropalmatine): These alkaloids in Corydalis yanhusuo capsules have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with neck pain.
Corybulbine: This alkaloid has analgesic and sedative effects, which can help to relieve pain and promote relaxation, making it easier to manage neck pain.
Corydaline: This alkaloid has analgesic and muscle relaxant properties, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and relieve pain associated with neck strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Corydalis yanhusuo's roots are primarily used due to their high concentration of berberine, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Barks: The barks of Corydalis yanhusuo are used to create capsules for neck pain due to their content of alkaloids that help to reduce pain and inflammation.
Leaves: Corydalis yanhusuo's leaves are used due to their rich content of alkaloids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relieve neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried corydalis yanhusuo root and 5 grams of herbal powder filler material.
Mix the dried root with the herbal powder filler material in a 1:1 ratio to create a uniform blend.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 capsules with the uniform blend at a dosage of 0.1 grams per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place to preserve their potency for up to 6 months.
Label the capsules with the dosage and ingredients and store them in a secure location for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen capsules helps with neck pain because they contain a powerful herb that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The capsules work by reducing inflammation in the neck muscles, which is often a major contributor to neck pain. By targeting the root cause of the discomfort, danshen capsules provide fast and effective relief from stiff, sore, and achy necks. Regular use of the capsules can also help to reduce muscle tension and improve overall range of motion, making it an excellent natural remedy for managing chronic neck pain.
remedy_properties: Salvianolic acids: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on nerves in the neck, leading to relief from neck pain.
Tanshinones: Tanshinones are known for their analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve muscle spasms and pain in the neck, reducing stiffness and discomfort.

Rosmarinic acid: This compound has been shown to have a positive effect on inflammation and oxidative stress, which may contribute to chronic neck pain; it also has a soothing and calming effect on the muscles and joints.
remedy_constituents: Tanshinone: Tanshinone has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the neck region, thereby alleviating neck pain.
Salvianolic acid A: Salvianolic acid A has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the neck muscles, leading to relief from neck pain.
Lithospermic acid: Lithospermic acid has vasodilatory properties that help improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, which can help alleviate tension and pain in the neck muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high concentration of compounds like tanshinones, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Stems: Utilized for their content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may contribute to their potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: Employed for their rich source of salvinolic acid and tanshinones, which are thought to have vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Dry the salvia miltiorrhiza root for 2 hours at 60 degrees celsius to remove moisture content.
Grind 30 grams of dried salvia miltiorrhiza root into a fine powder using a mill for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 10 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Encapsulate the mixture into gelatin capsules using a capsule machine at a production rate of 100 capsules per minute.
Label and package the capsules in airtight containers and store them in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica capsules helps with neck pain because they contain a combination of herbs that work together to relax muscle tension and reduce inflammation. The capsule's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm irritated nerves and joints, while its muscle-relaxing effects ease stiffness and spasms in the neck muscles. Additionally, angelica's natural sedative properties can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are often underlying causes of neck pain. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal angelica capsules provide effective relief from chronic neck pain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Angelica extracts have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to neck pain by reducing muscle spasms and joint stiffness.
Muscle Relaxant Properties: The coumarin compounds found in Angelica can relax muscle spasms, relieve tension, and ease pain associated with neck strain or whiplash injuries.

Analgesic and Sedative Properties: Angelica's volatile oils have been found to possess analgesic and sedative properties, which may help reduce pain and discomfort related to neck pain, as well as promote relaxation and improved sleep.
remedy_constituents: Fern-leaf lignans: Fern-leaf lignans, particularly sesamin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and pain associated with neck pain.
Sesquiterpene lactones: Sesquiterpene lactones, such as angelan, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help alleviate pain and discomfort in the neck and surrounding areas.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including ferulic acid, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the neck and shoulder muscles, thus providing relief from neck pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds like ferulic acid and polysaccharides that have anti-inflammatory properties and can help alleviate neck pain.
Leaves: Contain angelic acid and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and analgesic effects, making them useful for neck pain relief.
Stems: Contain ferulic acid and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and relieve neck pain, although less commonly used than roots and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested angelica archangelica in a temperature-controlled dehydrator at 95°f for 2-3 hours to preserve its properties.
Grind the dried angelica archangelica into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the angelica archangelica powder with 2 parts of a filler material such as starch or cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill 0.5-1 gram capsules with the angelica archangelica blend and seal them using a capsule filling machine or a manual capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony capsules helps with neck pain because they contain a potent combination of herbal extracts that work synergistically to reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and improve circulation. The peony root, in particular, has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to relieve joint and muscle pain, while other herbs such as angelica and licorice root contribute to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. By addressing the underlying causes of neck pain, herbal Chinese peony capsules can provide fast and effective relief from stiffness, soreness, and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The capsule's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with neck strain, making it an effective remedy for alleviating stiffness and discomfort.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic properties of the capsules work to block the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from neck pain caused by muscle spasms or strain.

Relaxant and Sedative: The herbal extract's relaxing and sedative properties help to calm muscle tension and promote relaxation, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing chronic neck pain and tension.
remedy_constituents: Paeoniflorin: This isoflavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with neck strain or injury.
Paeonol: A phenolic acid, paeonol is known for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can provide relief from neck pain caused by muscle tension or irritation.

Paenolide: A type of sesquiterpene lactone, paenolide has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Rhyzomes: Used due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Collect 30g of dried paeonia lactiflora roots and 10g of starch powder for capsule filling.
Grind 30g of dried paeonia lactiflora roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 10g of starch powder and add 5ml of water to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 300mg of the herbal powder mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place to maintain their potency for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules help with neck pain because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease stiffness in the affected area. The curcuminoid compounds present in turmeric work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing pain and discomfort. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect the body from further damage caused by oxidative stress, promoting overall health and well-being. As a result, taking herbal turmeric capsules can provide natural relief from neck pain and promote improved mobility and flexibility.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the neck muscles and joints, alleviating pain and stiffness.

Corticosteroid-Inhibiting Properties: Turmeric capsules containing curcumin have been shown to inhibit the production of corticosteroids, which are hormones that can exacerbate chronic pain and inflammation in the neck, allowing for a reduction in pain and discomfort.

Pain Relief via COX-2 Inhibition: Curcumin has been found to inhibit the activity of COX-2 (Cyclooxygenase 2), an enzyme involved in the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause pain and inflammation in the neck, providing natural relief from pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected area.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A curcuminoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contributes to reducing neck pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals.

Volatile oil (Turmerone): A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory properties, which helps alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the muscles and joints in the neck region.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes are used because they contain a high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: The roots are used because they are rich in curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate neck pain.
Stems: The stems are used because they contain a smaller amount of curcumin compared to the rhizomes and roots but still offer some anti-inflammatory benefits that can help alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried curcuma longa rhizomes from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Dry the rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried curcuma longa into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Mix 300mg of the powdered curcuma longa with 100mg of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with neck pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. When taken internally, the active compounds in ginger travel to the affected area, reducing swelling and easing stiffness in the neck muscles and joints. Additionally, ginger's warming effect can help increase blood flow and relax tense muscles, allowing for a greater range of motion and relief from chronic pain. This natural approach can be especially effective for those who experience recurring or persistent neck strain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal ginger capsules contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain in the neck area.

Pain-relieving properties: Ginger has natural analgesic and antinociceptive properties that can help block pain pathways and reduce pain perception, making it an effective natural remedy for neck pain relief.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to chronic neck pain conditions like tension headaches and migraines.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neck pain.
Shogaols: As a bioactive compound derived from gingerols, shogaols have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which contribute to their pain-relieving properties in neck pain.
Voltilin: Also known as Gingerol C-6, Voltilin has shown significant pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a key constituent in ginger capsules for alleviating neck pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate neck pain.
Roots: Roots are also widely used due to their ability to provide relief from muscle and joint pain, making them an effective remedy for neck pain.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used due to their potential to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce neck pain and promote overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Dry the zingiber officinale root in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered zingiber officinale with 250 milligrams of vegetable cellulose in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty capsules with the zingiber officinale and cellulose mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with neck pain because it has a natural anti-inflammatory property that reduces swelling and discomfort in the muscles and joints of the neck. The herb's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to relax tense muscles, ease stiffness, and improve blood circulation, which can help alleviate chronic pain and stiffness in the neck region. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce muscle spasms and tension that may be contributing to neck pain.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce neck pain and discomfort by reducing swelling and inflammation in the affected area.

Hypericum: The hypericum constituent in St. John's Wort has been found to have a direct effect on nerve function, which may help alleviate neck pain by blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain and promoting relaxation of tense muscles.
Adhyperforin: This compound has been shown to have a potent anti-inflammatory effect, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the neck region, leading to relief from pain and stiffness.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This terpene compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce inflammation and pain in the neck area.
Hypotensive compounds (including flavonoids and phenolic acids): These compounds have vasodilatory effects, which help improve blood flow and reduce muscle spasms in the neck, thus alleviating pain.
Hyperoside: This flavonoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the neck area, thereby providing relief from pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in hyperforin and hypericin, two compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Stems: Contain hyperforin and hypericin, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the neck area.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that could help reduce pain and inflammation in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a trusted source for medicinal use.
Mix 10 grams of the dried plant material with 10 grams of gelatin powder to create a mixture.
Add 50 ml of distilled water to the mixture and heat it for 15 minutes to dissolve gelatin.
Fill a capsule machine with the gelatin mixture and create 50-60 capsules with 200-250 mg each.
Label and date the capsules and store them in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog holly capsules helps with neck pain because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to reduce inflammation, relax tense muscles, and improve blood flow. The anti-inflammatory properties of the herbs help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the neck, while the muscle-relaxing effects alleviate stiffness and tension. Additionally, the improved blood flow brings oxygen and nutrients to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal dog holly capsules contain flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with neck pain.

Pain-relieving property: The capsules' analgesic properties, including the presence of alkaloids like berberine and palmatine, can help alleviate pain and discomfort in the neck and surrounding areas.
Muscle relaxant property: Herbal dog holly has been traditionally used to relax muscles, which can help reduce muscle spasms and tension that contribute to neck pain and stiffness.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Ruscus aculeatus capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain associated with neck strain.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly chlorogenic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to alleviate neck pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ruscus aculeatus capsules have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve neck pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals and reducing inflammation in the muscles and joints.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Ruscus aculeatus are used to make capsules for neck pain due to their purported anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Ruscus aculeatus are used to make capsules for neck pain due to their reported ability to reduce muscle spasms and inflammation.
Stems: The stems of Ruscus aculeatus are used to make capsules for neck pain due to their potential anti-arthritic properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried ruscus aculeatus root and 100 grams of gelatin for the capsule preparation.
Weigh out 2-3 grams of the dried ruscus aculeatus root and grind it into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 10-15 grams of gelatin powder in a bowl and stir until well combined.
Add 20-30 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and stir until the gelatin dissolves completely within 10 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill the gelatin mixture into empty vegetarian capsules, making 100-150 capsules within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with neck pain because of its unique properties that reduce inflammation and relax muscles. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, have potent anti-inflammatory effects that help to calm irritated tissues in the neck and shoulder area. Additionally, dandelion's natural diuretic properties help to relieve fluid retention, which can contribute to tension and stiffness in the neck muscles. By reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles, dandelion capsules can provide relief from chronic neck pain and improve overall joint mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the muscles and joints, including those in the neck, thereby alleviating pain.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of dandelion capsules may help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic pain and stiffness in the neck and other areas.

Pain-relieving flavonoids: Dandelion contains flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic (pain-relieving) and anti-inflammatory effects, making them potentially useful for managing neck pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and relieving pain associated with neck pain.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids and omega-3 fatty acids that help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can alleviate symptoms of neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for capsule production.
Mix 200 grams of dried taraxacum officinale herb with 100 grams of potato starch for a standardized extract ratio.
Grind the herbal mixture into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 250 milligrams of the powdered taraxacum officinale blend, sealing each capsule securely.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer-term preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with neck pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and calming properties. The herb contains apigenin, an antioxidant that reduces swelling and relaxes muscles, which eases tension in the neck and shoulders. Additionally, chamomile's natural sedative effects help to calm the mind and promote relaxation, reducing muscle spasms and stiffness. By taking herbal chamomile capsules regularly, individuals can experience relief from chronic neck pain, allowing them to enjoy improved mobility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile capsules contain apigenin, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation and swelling in the muscles and joints, helping to alleviate neck pain caused by inflammatory conditions such as arthritis.
Muscle relaxant: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenes have a calming effect on muscle spasms and tension, relaxing the neck muscles and reducing stiffness and pain associated with strain or injury.

Sedative and anti-anxiety properties: Chamomile's soothing effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to neck pain. By promoting relaxation and calmness, chamomile capsules can indirectly alleviate neck tension and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and relax muscles, making it effective in relieving neck pain.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm muscle tension causing neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Measure out 2000 milligrams of the dried flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered flowers with 100 milliliters of water to create a uniform decoction allowing it to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids retaining 500 milligrams of the herbal extract.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the strained herbal extract and seal them promptly storing them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree capsules helps with neck pain because of its ability to naturally reduce inflammation and alleviate muscle tension. The herb contains compounds that have been shown to have a potent anti-inflammatory effect, which can help to relax tense muscles and joints in the neck region. Additionally, chaste tree has been traditionally used to treat menstrual cramps and spasms, suggesting its potential to ease chronic pain associated with tightness or strain in the neck area. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, chaste tree capsules may offer a natural solution for individuals experiencing persistent neck pain.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Chasteberry (Vitex agnus-castus) capsules contain flavonoids and iridoid glycosides that can help dilate blood vessels, which may reduce inflammation and alleviate tension in the neck muscles.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's extracts have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, reducing swelling and pain associated with neck pain conditions such as whiplash or cervical spondylosis.

Muscle relaxant effects: Chasteberry capsules contain alkaloids that can help relax smooth muscle tissue, which may alleviate spasms and tension in the neck and shoulder muscles, common causes of neck pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chaste tree contain valuable compounds like flavonoids and iridoids, which help alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation.
Seeds: Chaste tree seeds have been traditionally used to treat musculoskeletal disorders, including neck pain, due to their ability to relax muscles and reduce stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried vitex agnus castus fruit and measure out 250 grams.
Grind the dried fruit into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground vitex powder with 100 grams of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the vitex-gelatin blend and seal them tightly.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with neck pain because they possess natural anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce muscle spasms and tension. The calming effects of lavender oil also promote relaxation, easing the stiffness and discomfort associated with neck pain. By targeting both physical and emotional factors, English lavender capsules provide a holistic approach to relieving neck strain, allowing individuals to enjoy improved mobility, reduced pain, and increased overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English Lavender capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation, which can contribute to neck pain by soothing irritated tissues and muscles, allowing for easier movement and reduced discomfort.

Pain relief: The lavender oil in these capsules contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate that work together to provide natural pain relief, helping to alleviate the sharpness or dull ache associated with neck pain.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing): Lavender has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation, both of which are often linked to neck pain; by reducing anxiety, English Lavender capsules can help alleviate neck pain caused or exacerbated by stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Lavandula angustifolia contain essential oils that help reduce inflammation and relax muscles, making them effective in alleviating neck pain.
Seeds: The seeds of English lavender are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that can be extracted to create a natural analgesic, providing relief from neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and sift them through a fine-mesh sieve to remove impurities.
Grind the dried lavender flowers into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle for 10-15 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the lavender powder with 100 ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container, steeping for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass measuring cup, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 mg of the lavender extract and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with neck pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules contain a concentrated form of Cinnamomum verum, which is rich in cinnamic acid and other bioactive compounds that reduce inflammation and relieve tension in the muscles. As neck pain often stems from muscle strain or inflammation, the herbal extract works to alleviate discomfort and stiffness, promoting relaxation and flexibility in the affected areas.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Cinnamon contains a type of polyphenol called cinnamaldehyde, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the neck.
Natural analgesic properties: Cinnamon's active compounds, including cinnamic acid, have been shown to exhibit analgesic (pain-relieving) properties, making it a natural remedy for easing neck pain.
Antioxidant activity: Ceylon cinnamon capsules contain high levels of antioxidants, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to chronic neck pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots of the Ceylon cinnamon plant have been traditionally used to relieve joint and muscle pain, making them suitable for neck pain relief.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Ceylon cinnamon plant are high in cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce neck pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum leaves in the morning after dew evaporation to ensure maximum essential oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or sun drying method within 24 hours of harvesting to preserve nutrients and flavor.
Crush the dried cinnamomum verum leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, then sift through a 100-mesh sieve for uniform particle size.
Mix 500 milligrams of the crushed leaf powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water to form a paste, then incubate for 30 minutes to enhance bioavailability.
Fill sterile capsules with 250 milligrams of the prepared paste and seal immediately, storing in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with neck pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in peppermint can help relax tense muscles, reduce stiffness, and ease tension in the neck, shoulders, and back. As a result, it provides rapid relief from acute and chronic neck pain, migraines, and headaches. Additionally, peppermint's soothing effects can also improve circulation, reduce swelling, and promote healing, further contributing to its effectiveness in managing neck pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal peppermint capsules can help reduce inflammation in the muscles and joints, which can contribute to neck pain, by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, hormones that cause swelling and pain.
Muscle relaxant: Peppermint oil has a calming effect on muscles, relaxing tense muscles and reducing spasms, which can relieve tension headaches and neck stiffness.
Pain reliever: The menthol in peppermint oil can help numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort associated with neck strain or arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other bioactive compounds, roots are commonly used to create capsules that provide fast-acting relief from neck pain.
Rhizomes: The underground stem of the peppermint plant, rhizomes contain a high concentration of essential oils and sesquiterpenes, making them an effective ingredient for reducing neck stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality mentha x piperita leaves and dry them thoroughly in the shade for at least 24 hours.
Combine one part dried mint leaves with three parts organic potato starch and blend into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Add 250mg of the powdered mixture to 1000mg of vegetable gelatin and mix well to form a uniform compound.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the compound mixture and seal them immediately using a capsule filling machine.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for longer shelf life.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the neck, thereby alleviating pain and stiffness.

Pain-relieving flavonoids: The herb is rich in flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to possess analgesic (pain-reducing) properties, helping to ease neck pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Purple coneflower's antioxidant properties may help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage caused by injury or inflammation in the neck, promoting healing and reducing the risk of chronic pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with neck pain.
Flowers: The flowers of the purple coneflower contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have analgesic properties that can help alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in the morning for optimal potency.
Grind 20 grams of dried echinacea roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 10 grams of ground echinacea roots with 5 grams of ground echinacea flowers in a small bowl.
Fill empty capsules with the echinacea mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency within 30 minutes.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with neck pain because they contain a unique blend of anti-inflammatory compounds, including echinacea and phenolic acids. These natural ingredients work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate tension in the muscles and joints of the neck, providing relief from stiffness, soreness, and discomfort. As the body's natural healing processes are supported, flexibility and range of motion in the neck are restored, making daily activities more comfortable and reducing the risk of chronic pain.

Elder capsules helps with neck pain because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease tension in the muscles and joints of the neck. The capsules' rich antioxidant properties also help to combat free radicals, which contribute to chronic inflammation and pain. Additionally, elder's natural analgesic and relaxing effects calm the nervous system, promoting a sense of relief and reducing muscle spasms, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating neck pain.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to neck pain and stiffness by combating free radicals and promoting a healthy inflammatory response.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in elder capsules have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help to relieve neck pain by reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Sambucol: Sambucol, a bioflavonoid found in elder berries, has been traditionally used to reduce pain and stiffness associated with rheumatic conditions such as arthritis, which can also affect the neck, making it a potential natural remedy for neck pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Elder are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and relieving neck pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Elder contain alkaloids that help relax muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from tension and stiffness associated with neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried sambucus nigra flowers in the morning for maximum potency and quality.
Grind 1 cup of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered flower with 200 ml of glycerin and 100 ml of distilled water to create an infusion.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes, then strain and filter it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter for 30 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 mg of the strained liquid and store in an airtight container.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for neck pain are a natural remedy that helps alleviate discomfort, stiffness, and inflammation associated with neck pain. They contain a blend of herbs such as Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Capsicum annuum, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Angelica sinensis, Achillea millefolium, Corydalis yanhusuo, Zanthoxylum bungeanum, and Paeonia lactiflora. If left untreated, neck pain can lead to sleep disturbances, decreased productivity, and reduced quality of life, making everyday tasks and activities challenging.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to neck pain that are treated with herbal capsules are those affecting the musculoskeletal system, such as lower back pain, sciatica, and fibromyalgia. Herbal supplements like turmeric, ginger, and willow bark have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate joint pain and stiffness, while other herbs like devil's claw and ashwagandha provide relaxation and reduce muscle tension. These herbal capsules offer a natural alternative to traditional pain medications.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with neck pain is Turmeric and Ginger capsules. Turmeric contains Curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces pain and inflammation in the neck. Ginger capsules provide additional anti-inflammatory benefits and can help relax tense muscles. Another essential capsule is Boswellia. It reduces pain and inflammation by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Adding Ashwagandha capsules can help alleviate stress and promote relaxation, further reducing neck pain.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Spine, herbal capsules, specifically Harpagophytum procumbens (Devil's claw), have been found to be effective in reducing neck pain. The study, which included ten trials, found strong evidence for short-term improvements in pain and rescue medication for daily doses standardized to 50 mg or 100 mg harpagoside. Specifically, two high-quality trials demonstrated that Harpagophytum procumbens was as effective as 12.5 mg per day of rofecoxib, a conventional pain medication, in reducing pain and improving function.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with neck pain because they contain active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles in the neck, providing relief from pain and stiffness. Additionally, ginger has natural analgesic and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate muscle spasms and cramps in the neck, making it easier to move and perform daily activities. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to a treatment plan for neck pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: They help reduce pain and inflammation in the neck by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are substances that cause pain and inflammation.
Shogaols: They have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce neck pain and stiffness by blocking the action of pain-causing chemicals in the body.

Terpenoids (specifically, Bisabolol): They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce neck pain and inflammation by neutralizing free radicals and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems store nutrients and have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the neck area.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation, making them a key ingredient in capsules for neck pain relief.
Buds: Zingiber officinale buds contain essential oils that help reduce inflammation and pain, making them a popular choice for capsules aimed at alleviating neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with clean hands for optimal quality.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried zingiber officinale into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of the ground powder with 500 milligrams of a natural filler in a bowl.
Fill each capsule shell with 250 milligrams of the mixture and seal with a capsule machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with neck pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease muscle tension in the neck region. The curcumin present in these capsules has natural pain-relieving and antioxidant properties that combat inflammation, making it an effective remedy for chronic neck pain. Regular consumption of Curcuma longa capsules may also enhance flexibility and mobility, allowing individuals to move freely and engage in daily activities without discomfort. It promotes overall well-being and reduces pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Helps alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that contribute to pain and discomfort.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): May help reduce neck pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes that contribute to pain and inflammation.
Bis-demethoxycurcumin (BDMC): May help alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to pain and discomfort in the neck region.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa as they contain the highest concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound that reduces inflammation and alleviates pain.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also used as they contain a significant amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help in reducing pain and inflammation associated with neck pain.
Stems: Stems of Curcuma longa are sometimes used as they contain smaller amounts of curcumin and other beneficial compounds that contribute to their pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried curcuma longa rhizomes that have been properly dried for 24 hours.
Grind the dried curcuma longa rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 2g of the curcuma longa powder with 1g of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 capsules with the powder mixture in about 10 minutes.
Store the curcuma longa capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum capsules helps with neck pain because they contain capsaicin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When taken orally, capsaicin is absorbed into the bloodstream and binds to pain receptors in the body, reducing the transmission of pain signals to the brain. As a result, capsaicin helps to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow to the neck area, providing relief from stiffness and discomfort associated with neck pain. This natural remedy promotes a sense of relaxation and eases neck pain.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: It helps with neck pain by inhibiting the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Quercetin: It helps with neck pain by reducing inflammation and alleviating pain through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Ferulic acid: It helps with neck pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to neck pain and stiffness.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Capsicum annuum fruits (commonly known as peppers) are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate neck pain.
Seeds: Capsicum annuum seeds are used due to their high concentration of capsaicin, a compound that reduces pain and inflammation in the neck region.
Leaves: Capsicum annuum leaves are used due to their high antioxidant content and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce neck pain and improve overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum fruits at their ripest stage when color has fully developed.
Dry the harvested capsicum annuum fruits in a warm air circulation oven at 50 degrees celsius.
Grind 20 grams of dried capsicum annuum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 2 grams of the ground capsicum annuum powder with 2 grams of gelatin powder in a mortar.
Encapsulate the mixture into vegetarian capsules using a capsule filling machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with neck pain because they contain compounds that improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The anti-inflammatory properties of Ginkgo biloba help to relax tense muscles and reduce stiffness in the neck. Additionally, it increases oxygenation of the tissues, promoting faster healing and reducing pain. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba capsules may help to alleviate chronic neck pain, allowing for improved mobility and reduced discomfort. It may also enhance overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected areas, which can contribute to relief from neck pain.
Terpenoids: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and promote healing in the neck muscles and joints.
Bilobalide: It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort in the neck and surrounding areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part of the Ginkgo biloba plant due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help alleviate neck pain.

Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are often used in capsules as they contain a high amount of bilobalide and ginkgolides, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce neck pain.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree are sometimes used in capsules due to their potential to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation, which may contribute to the alleviation of neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Grind 250 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a food processor for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 100 grams of gelatin and 100 milliliters of distilled water in a bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes while stirring constantly with a wooden spoon.
Pour the mixture into a gelatin capsule mold and allow it to cool and set for 30 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled mixture using a capsule filling machine for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in the licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin, work to reduce swelling and ease stiffness in the muscles and joints. This natural anti-inflammatory effect helps to alleviate pain and discomfort in the neck, promoting relaxation and flexibility. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules may also help to improve range of motion and reduce the risk of further strain, making it a popular natural remedy for neck pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This constituent has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the neck muscles.
Liquiritigenin: This flavonoid has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate pain and discomfort in the neck area.
Licoricidin: This isoflavane has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the neck muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate neck pain.
Roots: Used for their antioxidant properties to reduce oxidative stress contributing to neck pain.
Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory glycosides, particularly glycyrrhizin, to help reduce inflammation and pain in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Obtain 250 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier and store in an airtight container.
Grind 100 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Sift 50 grams of the powder through a 40-mesh sieve to remove any large particles and lumps.
Mix the sifted powder with 20 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of stearic acid in a stainless steel mixing bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 to 1 gram of the herbal powder mixture and seal immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis capsules helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The capsules, made from the dried root of the Angelica plant, have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to relieve pain and stiffness. The active compounds in Angelica sinensis, such as ferulic acid and ligustilide, work to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making it an effective natural remedy for neck pain and related muscle tension. This results in improved mobility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: They help reduce inflammation and promote the repair of damaged tissues in the neck, alleviating pain and discomfort.
Ligustilide: It has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling in the neck by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals.

Butylphthalide: It has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling in the neck by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals and promoting the repair of damaged tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including ferulic acid and ligustilide, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Rhyzomes: They are also used because they contain similar bioactive compounds as the roots, providing relief from neck pain and inflammation.
Barks: They are sometimes used due to their secondary metabolites that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, although they are less concentrated than roots and rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried angelica sinensis root by selecting fresh roots with a rich green color.
Dry the harvested angelica sinensis root in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried angelica sinensis root into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix the powdered angelica sinensis root with 50 grams of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Encapsulate the gelatin mixture in empty capsules using a semi-automatic capsule filling machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium capsules helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb, also known as yarrow, has been traditionally used to relieve muscle and joint discomfort. Its active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the neck area. By reducing swelling and promoting relaxation, Achillea millefolium capsules can provide relief from neck pain, promoting flexibility and range of motion, and improving overall well-being. Regular use may also help prevent future episodes of neck pain.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the neck muscles and joints, thereby alleviating pain.
Phenolic acids: They exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities, which help block pain signals and reduce inflammation in the neck region, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the neck muscles and joints from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing pain and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and discomfort associated with neck strain.
Flowers: The flowers are often used as they contain flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that ease neck pain.
Roots: The roots are typically used because they contain sesquiterpenes and polyacetylenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 stems of achillea millefolium, selecting those with good quality leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon.
Dry the harvested stems in a single layer, away from direct sunlight, at a temperature of 35-40°c for 2-3 hours.
Crush 1-2 tablespoons of dried leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powdered herb with 1 teaspoon of gelatin in a small bowl, adding a few drops of water to moisten the mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, sealing them properly to maintain the potency of the herbal extract.

remedies.remedy_desc: Corydalis yanhusuo capsules helps with neck pain because of its unique combination of analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds, such as dehydrocorybulbine (DHCB), have been shown to inhibit the production of pain-producing chemicals in the body, providing rapid relief from neck pain. Additionally, Corydalis yanhusuo has a long history of use in traditional Chinese medicine to relieve pain and discomfort associated with musculoskeletal conditions, making it a popular natural remedy for neck pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Protopine: It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Corydaline: This alkaloid constituent has analgesic and muscle relaxant properties, which help to relieve muscle tension and spasms in the neck, thereby reducing pain.
Yanhuangine: It has a sedative and anti-anxiety effect, which helps to calm the mind and body, reducing muscle tension and alleviating neck pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their bioactive compounds, such as tetrandrine and berberine, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used due to their content of bioactive compounds, including corydaline, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60g of corydalis yanhusuo roots with a diameter of 1-2cm and cut them into small pieces.
Dry the small pieces in the shade at room temperature for 7-10 days or by using a dehydrator.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the fine powder with 200mg of starch and 200mg of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill the mixture into gelatin capsules and store them in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zanthoxylum bungeanum capsules helps with neck pain because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active ingredients in the capsules, such as ferulic acid and caryophyllene, work together to reduce swelling and ease pain in the neck area. By reducing inflammation and alleviating pain, Zanthoxylum bungeanum capsules provide relief from neck pain, allowing individuals to move freely and comfortably without discomfort or stiffness. This natural remedy offers a soothing solution for those suffering from neck pain.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with neck pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to pain and discomfort in the neck area.
Coumarins: They help with neck pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can provide relief from pain and swelling in the neck and surrounding areas.
Lignans: They help with neck pain by acting as a natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation in the neck and improve overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with neck pain.
Stems: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the neck area.
Leaves: Employed due to their ability to relax muscles and reduce tension, providing relief from neck pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh or dried zanthoxylum bungeanum leaves and flowers in quantities of 500 grams.
Dry the collected plant material in a hot air oven at 50 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Grind the dried zanthoxylum bungeanum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder in doses of 300 milligrams and seal the capsules.
Store the herbal capsules in a cool dry place for up to two years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora capsules helps with neck pain because they contain paeoniflorin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and inflammation in the neck muscles, thereby relieving tension and pain. Additionally, paeoniflorin has analgesic properties that numb the pain, providing quick relief from discomfort. The herb also exhibits a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to relax tense muscles and improve blood circulation, which further contributes to its pain-relieving effects. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective solution for managing neck pain.
remedy_constituents: Paeoniflorin: This glycoside helps reduce inflammation and pain in the neck by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and neurotransmitters.
Paeonol: This phenolic compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and swelling in the neck muscles and joints.

Paeonol acetate: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in the neck by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and free radicals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Paeonia lactiflora roots are used to make capsules for neck pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Paeonia lactiflora flowers are used to make capsules for neck pain due to their analgesic and sedative effects.
Rhyzomes: Paeonia lactiflora rhyzomes are used to make capsules for neck pain due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried roots from paeonia lactiflora plants during the autumn season when mature.
Dry the harvested roots at 50-60°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture completely.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 2 grams of the powdered paeonia lactiflora root with 1 gram of cellulose powder in a bowl thoroughly.
Fill the powder mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a powder filler or spoon for 10 minutes.
